REGIONAL TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER - RTSP INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0858 UTC, Wednesday, 11 April 2012
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION BULLETIN ...
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (preliminary)
RTSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following preliminary
information:
Magnitude : 8.3 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 11 Apr 2012
Origin Time: 08:38:00 UTC
Latitude : 2.33N
Longitude : 93.04E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
RTSP INDONESIA is evaluating this earthquake to determine if a tsunami has
been generated.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local authorities
and disaster management officers have the authority to make decisions regarding
the official threat and warning status in their coastal areas and any action to
be taken in response.
4. UPDATES
Additional bulletins will be issued by RTSP INDONESIA for this event as more
information becomes available.
Other RTSPs may issue additional information at:
RTSP AUSTRALIA: http://reg.bom.gov.au/tsunami/rtsp/
RTSP INDIA: http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp
In case of conflicting information from RTSPs or the IAS (PTWC, JMA), the more
conservative information should be used for safety.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 2 (TYPE-II)
REGIONAL TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER - RTSP INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0903 UTC, Wednesday, 11 April 2012
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (preliminary)
RTSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
information:
Magnitude : 8.3 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 11 Apr 2012
Origin Time: 08:38:00 UTC
Latitude : 2.33N
Longitude : 93.04E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
Earthquakes of this size are capable of generating tsunamis. However, so far
there is no confirmation about the triggering of a tsunami.
An investigation is under way to determine if a tsunami has been triggered.
This RTSP will monitor sea level gauges and report if any tsunami wave activity
has occurred.
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local authorities
and disaster management officers have the authority to make decisions regarding
the official threat and warning status in their coastal areas and any action to
be taken in response.
4. UPDATES
Additional bulletins will be issued by RTSP INDONESIA for this event as more
information becomes available.
Other RTSPs may issue additional information at:
RTSP AUSTRALIA: http://reg.bom.gov.au/tsunami/rtsp/
RTSP INDIA: http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp
In case of conflicting information from RTSPs or the IAS (PTWC, JMA), the more
conservative information should be used for safety.
5. CONTACT INFORMATION

REGIONAL TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER - RTSP INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 1906 UTC, Wednesday, 11 April 2012
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
RTSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following FINAL
information:
Magnitude : 8.3 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 11 Apr 2012
Origin Time: 08:38:00 UTC
Latitude : 2.33N
Longitude : 93.04E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified locations.
Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal sea level; it is NOT
the crest-to-trough wave height.
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Sabang INDONESIA 05.80N 95.00E 0.06 10:00 Apr 11, 2012
Meulaboh INDONESIA 04.32N 96.22E 0.8 10:04 Apr 11, 2012
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local authorities
and disaster management officers have the authority to make decisions regarding
the official threat and warning status in their coastal areas and any action to
be taken in response.
5. UPDATES

information becomes available.

REGIONAL TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER - RTSP INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0615 UTC, Wednesday, 12 June 2013
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (preliminary)
RTSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
information:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 12 Jun 2013
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 12.65N
Longitude : 93.50E
Location : Andaman Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
Earthquakes of this size are capable of generating tsunamis. However, so far
there is no confirmation about the triggering of a tsunami.
An investigation is under way to determine if a tsunami has been triggered.
This RTSP will monitor sea level gauges and report if any tsunami wave activity
has occurred.
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local authorities
and disaster management officers have the authority to make decisions regarding
the official threat and warning status in their coastal areas and any action to
be taken in response.
4. UPDATES
Additional bulletins will be issued by RTSP INDONESIA for this event as more
information becomes available.
Other RTSPs may issue additional information at:
RTSP AUSTRALIA: http://reg.bom.gov.au/tsunami/rtsp/
RTSP INDIA: http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp
In case of conflicting information from RTSPs or the IAS (PTWC, JMA), the more
conservative information should be used for safety.
5. CONTACT INFORMATION

information:

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 12.65N
Longitude : 93.50E
Location : Andaman Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS

Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal sea level; it is NOT
the crest-to-trough wave height.

evaluation_text:
2. EVALUATION
Earthquakes of this size are capable of generating tsunamis. However, so far
there is no confirmation about the triggering of a tsunami.
An investigation is under way to determine if a tsunami has been triggered.
This RTSP will monitor sea level gauges and report if any tsunami wave activity
has occurred.

Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.

continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

information:

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N

has occurred.

evaluation_text:
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified locations.
Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal sea level; it is NOT
the crest-to-trough wave height.
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Jask Iran 25.63N 57.77E 6.3 06:04 Jun 10, 2015
Gwadar Pakistan 25.13N 62.03E 7.0 06:15 Jun 10, 2015
Muscat Oman 23.63N 58.57E 4.5 06:20 Jun 10, 2015
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

information:

Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.

CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

evaluation_text:
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on RTSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified locations.
Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal sea level; it is NOT
the crest-to-trough wave height.
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CampbellBay INDIA 6.90N 93.74E 11.0 0604z 09 Dec 2015
Nancowry INDIA 7.96N 93.53E 10.0 0615z 09 Dec 2015
Sabang INDONESIA 5.83N 95.33E 9.0 0626Z 09 Dec 2015
Meulaboh INDONESIA 4.32N 96.22E 11.0 0627Z 09 Dec 2015
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (preliminary)

information:
Magnitude : 7.8 (Mw)
Depth : 10km

Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean. It is issued in support of
the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 8.5 (Mw)
Depth : 10km
Date : 08 Jun 2016
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 12.65N
Longitude : 93.50E
Location : Andaman Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified locations.
Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal sea level; it is NOT
the crest-to-trough wave height.

and disaster management officers have the authority to make decisions regarding
the official threat and warning status in their coastal areas and any action to
be taken in response.

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean. It is issued in support of
the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.2 (Mw)
Depth : 10km
Date : 07 Sep 2016
Origin Time: 03:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 1.93S
Longitude : 99.22E
Location : Southern Sumatra, Indonesia
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 4 (TYPE-III)
IOTWS TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0700 UTC, Thursday, 08 September 2016
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... CONFIRMED TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (preliminary)
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
information:
Magnitude : 9.0 (Mw)
Depth : 10km
Date : 08 Sep 2016
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 62.20E
Location : Off Coast of Pakistan
2. EVALUATION
Sea level observations have confirmed that a TSUNAMI WAS GENERATED and
there MAY BE A THREAT TO SOME INDIAN OCEAN COUNTRIES.
Maximum wave amplitudes observed so far:
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHABAHAR IRAN 25.29N 60.60E 8.0 06:30 Sep 08, 2016
MUSCAT OMAN 23.63N 58.57E 5.0 06:40 Sep 08, 2016
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWS member states. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs for
their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
4. UPDATES
Additional bulletins will be issued by IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA for this event as
more information becomes available.
Other IOTWS-TSPs may issue additional information at:
IOTWS-TSP AUSTRALIA: http://www.bom.gov.au/tsunami/iotws/
IOTWS-TSP INDIA : http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp
5. CONTACT INFORMATION
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA:
THE AGENCY FOR METEOROLOGY CLIMATOLOGY AND GEOPHYSICS (BMKG)
InaTEWS - Indonesian Tsunami Early Warning System
Address: Jl. Angkasa I no.2 Kemayoran, Jakarta, Indonesia, 10720
Tel.: +62 (21) 4246321/6546316
Fax: +62 (21) 6546316/4246703
P.O. Box 3540 Jakarta
Website: http://www.bmkg.go.id
http://inatews.bmkg.go.id
E-Mail: inartsp@bmkg.go.id
monitrtwp@bmkg.go.id
6. TSUNAMI WARNING SYSTEMS OUTSIDE THE INDIAN OCEAN
Pacific Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (PTWS):
----------------------------------------------------
Pacific Tsunami Warning Centre (PTWC)
http://ptwc.weather.gov/
North West Pacific Tsunami Advisory Centre (NWPTAC)
http://www.jma.go.jp/en/tsunami/
US National Tsunami Warning Centre (US NTWC)
http://wcatwc.arh.noaa.gov/
Joint Australian Tsunami Warning Centre (JATWC)
http://www.bom.gov.au/tsunami/

--------------------------------------------------------------

http://www.koeri.boun.edu.tr/2/en/

-------------------------------------------

http://ptwc.weather.gov/

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean. It is issued in support of
the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 (Mw)
Depth : 10km
Date : 08 Sep 2016
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 62.20E
Location : Off Coast of Pakistan
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean. It is issued in support of
the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 (Mw)
Depth : 10km
Date : 14 Dec 2016
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 7.20N
Longitude : 92.90E
Location : Nicobar Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all threatened
Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave action,
CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination must be made by
national/state/local authorities. Please be aware that dangerous currents can
continue for several hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS

the crest-to-trough wave height.

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.2 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 13 Dec 2017
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N
Longitude : 95.96E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS

locations. Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal
sea level; it is NOT the crest-to-trough wave height.

Latitude : 6.94S
Longitude : 104.70E

Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.

1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)

Depth : 10km

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 58.20E
Location : Gulf of Oman
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... EARTHQUAKE BULLETIN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean
and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
For information applying to areas outside the Indian Ocean refer to
the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following
preliminary information:
Magnitude : 8.5 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 05 Sep 2018
Origin Time: 03:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N
Longitude : 96.00E
Location : Northern Sumatra, Indonesia
2. EVALUATION
Based on historical data and tsunami modelling, this earthquake
may be capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA will monitor the situation to determine
if a tsunami was generated and will issue further bulletins as
information becomes available.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
Indian Ocean countries. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs
For their countries can be found at:

... EARTHQUAKE BULLETIN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean
and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
For information applying to areas outside the Indian Ocean refer to
the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following
preliminary information:
Magnitude : 9.1 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 12 Dec 2018
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 10.40S
Longitude : 112.80E
Location : South of Java, Indonesia
2. EVALUATION
Based on historical data and tsunami modelling, this earthquake
may be capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA will monitor the situation to determine
if a tsunami was generated and will issue further bulletins as
information becomes available.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
Indian Ocean countries. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs
For their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
4. OTHER INDIAN OCEAN TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDERS:
Other IOTWMS-TSPs may issue additional information at:

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.1 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 12 Dec 2018
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 10.40S
Longitude : 112.80E
Location : South of Java, Indonesia
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified
locations. Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal
sea level; it is NOT the crest-to-trough wave height.

Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).

Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.

action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning

PUBLIC TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 1
IOTWMS TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 1214 UTC, Friday, 02 August 2019
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... EARTHQUAKE BULLETIN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean
and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
For information applying to areas outside the Indian Ocean refer to
the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following
preliminary information:
Magnitude : 7.0 Mwp
Depth : 84km
Date : 02 Aug 2019
Origin Time: 12:03:23 UTC
Latitude : 7.58S
Longitude : 104.53E
Location : Southwest of Sumatra, Indonesia
2. EVALUATION
Based on historical data and tsunami modelling, this earthquake
may be capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA will monitor the situation to determine
if a tsunami was generated and will issue further bulletins as
information becomes available.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
Indian Ocean countries. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs
For their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
4. OTHER INDIAN OCEAN TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDERS:
Other IOTWMS-TSPs may issue additional information at:
IOTWMS-TSP AUSTRALIA: http://www.bom.gov.au/tsunami/iotws
IOTWMS-TSP INDIA: http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp

... POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...

It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).

IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N

2. EVALUATION
Based on pre-run model scenarios, there is a POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT
to countries in the Indian Ocean.
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA will monitor sea level gauges and report if any tsunami
wave activity has occurred.
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs for
their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
4. UPDATES
Additional bulletins will be issued by IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA for this event as

PUBLIC TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 1
IOTWMS TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0756 UTC, Wednesday, 03 June 2020
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... EARTHQUAKE BULLETIN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean
and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
For information applying to areas outside the Indian Ocean refer to
the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following
preliminary information:
Magnitude : 6.6 Mwp
Depth : 84km
Date : 03 Jun 2020
Origin Time: 07:35:33 UTC
Latitude : 23.30S
Longitude : 68.48W
Location : Northern Chile
2. EVALUATION
Based on historical data and tsunami modelling, this earthquake
is not capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. No further bulletins will be issued unless the situation
changes.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
Indian Ocean countries. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs
For their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
4. OTHER INDIAN OCEAN TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDERS:
Other IOTWMS-TSPs may issue additional information at:
IOTWMS-TSP AUSTRALIA: http://www.bom.gov.au/tsunami/iotws
IOTWMS-TSP INDIA: http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp
5. CONTACT INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA:
THE AGENCY FOR METEOROLOGY CLIMATOLOGY AND GEOPHYSICS (BMKG)

Address: Jl. Angkasa I no.2 Kemayoran, Jakarta, Indonesia, 10720

... POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PUBLIC TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 4
IOTWMS TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0645 UTC, Wednesday, 10 June 2020
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 10 Jun 2020
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 7.20N
Longitude : 92.90E
Location : Nicobar Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination

and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).

the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.

preliminary information:
Magnitude : 8.5 Mwp

Date : 20 Oct 2020
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 62.20E
Location : Off Coast of Pakistan

may be capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA will monitor the situation to determine
if a tsunami was generated and will issue further bulletins as
information becomes available.

http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id

evaluation_text:
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified
locations. Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal
sea level; it is NOT the crest-to-trough wave height.
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Sebesi Island INDONESIA 5.94S 105.51E 13.3 06:00 Jun 09, 2021
Marina Jambu INDONESIA 6.19S 105.84E 9.3 06:00 Jun 09, 2021
Christmas Island AUSTRALIA 10.43S 105.67E 11.8 06:15 Jun 09, 2021
Pangandaran INDONESIA 7.75S 108.50E 10.7 06:19 Jun 09, 2021
Cilacap INDONESIA 7.75S 109.00E 7.4 06:26 Jun 09, 2021
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Latitude : 10.40S
Longitude : 112.80E
Location : South of Java, Indonesia

Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.2 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 08 Jun 2022
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N
Longitude : 95.96E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra

Sea level observations have confirmed that a TSUNAMI WAS GENERATED and
there MAY BE A THREAT TO SOME INDIAN OCEAN COUNTRIES.
Maximum wave amplitudes observed so far:

bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N
Longitude : 95.96E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.

action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

evaluation_text:
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified
locations. Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal
sea level; it is NOT the crest-to-trough wave height.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NANCOWRY INDIA 8.05N 93.55E 5.1 06:28 June 7, 2023

authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to

their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.

bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp (UPDATED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 04 Oct 2023
Origin Time: 0400 UTC
Latitude : 7.20N
Longitude : 92.90E
Location : Nicobar, India
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp (UPDATED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 11 Oct 2023
Origin Time: 0600 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 58.20E
Location : Gulf of Oman
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.

action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning

It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami

2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... POTENTIAL TSUNAMI THREAT IN THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km

Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 6.94S

to countries in the Indian Ocean.

Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.

action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning

PUBLIC TSUNAMI BULLETIN NUMBER 4
IOTWMS TSUNAMI SERVICE PROVIDER INDONESIA (InaTEWS-BMKG)
issued at 0645 UTC, Jun 11, 2025
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION (updated)
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp (REVISED)
Depth : 10km
Date : 11 Jun 2025
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 7.20N
Longitude : 92.90E
Location : Nicobar Islands, India Region
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.
3. TSUNAMI WAVE OBSERVATIONS
Listed below are maximum wave amplitudes recorded at the specified
locations. Note that wave amplitude is measured relative to normal
sea level; it is NOT the crest-to-trough wave height.
LOCATION COUNTRIES LAT LON AMPL(m) TIME(UTC) DATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NANCOWRY INDIA 8.05N 93.55E 23.6 06:26 Jun 11, 2025
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs for
their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
5. UPDATES
No further bulletins will be issued by IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA for this event
unless additional information becomes available.
Other IOTWMS-TSPs may issue additional information at:
IOTWS-TSP AUSTRALIA: http://www.bom.gov.au/tsunami/iotws/
IOTWS-TSP INDIA : http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/eqevents.jsp

2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.0 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 15 Oct 2025
Origin Time: 06:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 24.80N
Longitude : 62.20E
Location : Off Coast of Pakistan
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

... EARTHQUAKE BULLETIN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean
and is issued by Tsunami Service Provider INDONESIA in support of the
UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
For information applying to areas outside the Indian Ocean refer to
the relevant Tsunami Warning and Mitigation Systems listed in section
6 below.
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following
preliminary information:
Magnitude : 6.6 Mwp
Depth : 18km
Date : 16 Oct 2025
Origin Time: 05:48:53 UTC
Latitude : 1.94S
Longitude : 139.03E
Location : Near North Coast of West Papua
2. EVALUATION
Based on historical data and tsunami modelling, this earthquake
is not capable of generating a tsunami affecting the Indian Ocean
region. No further bulletins will be issued unless the situation
changes.
Further information on this event will be available at:
http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id
3. ADVICE
This bulletin is being issued as advice. Only national/state/local
authorities and disaster management officers have the authority to
make decisions regarding the official threat and warning status in
their coastal areas and any action to be taken in response.
For more detailed information, please refer to the tsunami advisory
bulletins issued by the National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
Indian Ocean countries. The tsunami warning status reported by NTWCs
For their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp

... FINAL TSUNAMI BULLETIN FOR THE INDIAN OCEAN ...
This bulletin applies to areas within and bordering the Indian Ocean.
It is issued in support of the UNESCO/IOC Indian Ocean Tsunami
Warning and Mitigation System (IOTWMS).
1. EARTHQUAKE INFORMATION
IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA has detected an earthquake with the following details:
Magnitude : 9.2 Mwp
Depth : 10km
Date : 05 Nov 2025
Origin Time: 03:00:00 UTC
Latitude : 3.30N
Longitude : 95.96E
Location : Off West Coast of Northern Sumatra
2. EVALUATION
Data from sea-level gauges confirmed that a tsunami was generated.
The expected period of significant tsunami waves is now over for all
Threatened Indian Ocean countries, based on IOTWMS-TSP INDONESIA modelling.
Because local conditions can cause a wide variation in tsunami wave
action, CANCELLATION of national warnings and ALL CLEAR determination
must be made by national/state/local authorities. The tsunami warning
status reported by National Tsunami Warning Centres (NTWCs) of
IOTWMS member states for their countries can be found at:
http://www.incois.gov.in/Incois/tsunami/NTWCFeedbackStatus.jsp
Please be aware that dangerous currents can continue for several
hours after the main tsunami waves have passed.

preliminary information:
